assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 reads the Last-Updated timestamp from the security-matrix.md HTML comment and parses the ISO 8601 value
assertion_results.evidence: The output file staleness-check.md shows parsed ISO 8601 timestamps for both streams: "Last-Updated timestamp: 2026-05-01T10:00:00Z" (lines 10 and 28). The heading confirms this is Step 0.3 ("# Step 0.3 -- Matrix Staleness Check"). The timestamps are presented in full ISO 8601 format, indicating they were read and parsed from the security-matrix.md files.

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 detects that the matrix is older than the 14-day default threshold and displays a staleness warning
assertion_results.evidence: For both streams, the output shows: Threshold: 14 days, Age: 63 days, Status: STALE. Staleness warnings are displayed as blockquotes: "Security matrix for stream **2.1.x** was last updated on 2026-05-01 (63 days ago). The matrix may not reflect recent releases." (lines 16-17 and 37-38).

assertion_results.text: The staleness warning includes the stream name, the last-updated date, and the number of days since the last update
assertion_results.evidence: Each warning blockquote includes all three elements: stream name ("stream **2.1.x**" and "stream **2.2.x**"), last-updated date ("last updated on 2026-05-01"), and number of days ("63 days ago"). See lines 16-17 and 37-38 of staleness-check.md.

assertion_results.text: The warning presents three options: refresh now (re-run setup Step 10.6), proceed anyway, or stop
assertion_results.evidence: Each warning includes three numbered options: "1. **Refresh now** -- re-run matrix population (setup Step 10.6) for this stream", "2. **Proceed anyway** -- continue triage with the current matrix", "3. **Stop** -- halt triage so I can investigate" (lines 19-22 and 40-43 of staleness-check.md).

assertion_results.text: Step 0.3 runs before Step 0.5 (JIRA Access Initialization) — no Jira operations are attempted before the staleness check
assertion_results.evidence: The only output file is staleness-check.md which covers Step 0.3. The output ends with "Awaiting user choice before proceeding" and "User confirmation is required for each stream before triage can proceed to Step 1" (lines 24, 45, 51). There is no evidence of any Jira operations (no Jira API calls, no Step 0.5 output, no issue fetching). The workflow halted at Step 0.3 awaiting user input, confirming it runs before any Jira access.
